Profile

About Me

I am an M.Sc. Electromobility candidate at Friedrich Alexander University Erlangen-Nürnberg,Germany currently completing my thesis at Fraunhofer LBF, Darmstadt on machine learning-based sorting of used Li-Ion cells using Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy.

My work sits at the intersection of battery diagnostics, data science, and sustainable engineering — combining deep knowledge of Li-Ion electrochemistry with hands-on ML model development to solve real second-life and recycling challenges in the EV battery supply chain.

Before my master’s, I spent three years in industry across quality engineering and project management, gaining a production-scale perspective that makes me equally comfortable in the lab and on the factory floor.

Journey

Experience & Projects

Ongoing · Master’s Thesis
ML-Based Sorting of Used Li-Ion Cells using EIS
Fraunhofer LBF, Darmstadt · June 2026
Developing a data-driven ML pipeline to automatically classify and sort used EV Li-Ion cells using Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy data, directly supporting Fraunhofer LBF’s automated battery Sorting system. Benchmarking CNN, GPR, and XGBoost architectures; investigating EIS data representations to optimise sorting accuracy. Validating model generalizability across varying SoH levels, temperatures, and cell chemistries using PFI and SHAP for interpretability.
